title: Stelis dewildei
intro: Stelis dewildei, also known as the "Hawaiian Orchid," is a flowering plant species in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis dewildei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Stelis dewildei is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, with some herbal preparations also available in extract or capsule form.
taxonomy: Stelis dewildei is a species of orchid native to Colombia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. This species is classified under the genus Stelis, which comprises approximately 900 species of epiphytic orchids. Stelis dewildei is further divided into various subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. The classification of Stelis dewildei is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of orchid taxonomy.
